# Growing Vegetables In Containers

Container gardening lets you grow vegetables anywhere, even on a small balcony. With the right pots, soil, and sunlight, you can harvest fresh tomatoes, peppers, and lettuce all season long without a backyard. This guide covers everything you need to start.

## Choosing containers

Pick containers at least 12 inches deep for most vegetables. Drainage holes are essential.

## Best vegetables for containers

Tomatoes, peppers, lettuce, radishes, and herbs all thrive in pots.

## Soil and watering

Use a quality potting mix, never garden soil. Water when the top inch feels dry.
